mission.title: kcp: Epic: reverse tunnel to pclusters through syncer for pod subresources
mission.description: Epic: reverse tunnel to pclusters through syncer for pod subresources. This issue affects 1+ users.

steps.title: Identify kcp feature symptoms
steps.description:
Check for the issue in your kcp deployment:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n cert-manager -l app=kcp
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=kcp -n cert-manager --tail=100 | grep -i error
```
Look for errors related to: Epic: reverse tunnel to pclusters through syncer for pod subresources

steps.title: Check current Pod configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant kcp resources:
```bash
kubectl get pod -A
kubectl describe pod <name> -n <namespace>
```
The kcp apiserver has to talk to the physical cluster for `kubectl exec|attach|logs` because pods are running there through the syncer.

# Demo Objective

  
Note: it might be ok for v0.7 to do `kubec

steps.title: Apply the fix for Epic: reverse tunnel to pclusters through syncer for pod sub
steps.description:
## Summary

Add functionality to the Syncer to establish reverse connections and act as "reverse proxy for the downstream clusters.

The KCP server exposes 2 URLs that allows to proxy over reverse connections with the following format
- `https://host/services/syncer-tunnels/clusters/<ws>/apis/worklo
```yaml
kubectl --server=https://{host}/services/syncer-tunnels/clusters/<ws>/apis/workload.kcp.dev/v1alpha1/synctargets/<name>/proxy get deployments -A
NAMESPACE                      NAME                           READY   UP-TO-DATE   AVAILABLE   AGE
kcp-syncer-cluster1-195k2b7i   kcp-syncer-cluster1-195k2b7i   1/1     1            1           34m
kube-system                    coredns                        2/2     2            2           4h53m
local-path-storage             local-path-provisioner         1/1     1            1           4h53m
```

steps.title: Confirm Epic: reverse tunnel to pclusters through syncer f is resolved
steps.description:
Verify the fix by checking that the original error no longer occurs:
```bash
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=kcp -n cert-manager --tail=50 --since=5m
kubectl get events -n cert-manager --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm that the issue symptoms are gone.

resolution.summary:
The root cause is: ## Summary

Add functionality to the Syncer to establish reverse connections and act as "reverse proxy for the downstream clusters.

The KCP server exposes 2 URLs that allows to proxy over reverse connections with the following format
- `https://host/services/syncer-tunnels/clusters/<ws>/apis/workload.kcp.dev/v1alpha1/synctargets/<name>/connect` : connections received in this URL are hold used to . This fixes the issue because it addresses the underlying problem that was causing the failure.
codeSnippets:
kubectl --server=https://{host}/services/syncer-tunnels/clusters/<ws>/apis/workload.kcp.dev/v1alpha1/synctargets/<name>/proxy get deployments -A
NAMESPACE                      NAME                           READY   UP-TO-DATE   AVAILABLE   AGE
kcp-syncer-cluster1-195k2b7i   kcp-syncer-cluster1-195k2b7i   1/1     1            1           34m
kube-system                    coredns                        2/2     2            2           4h53m
local-path-storage             local-path-provisioner         1/1     1            1           4h53m

kubectl --server=https://{host}/services/syncer-tunnels/clusters/<ws>/apis/workload.kcp.dev/v1alpha1/synctargets/<name>/proxy get pods -A
NAMESPACE                      NAME                                            READY   STATUS    RESTARTS   AGE
kcp-syncer-cluster1-195k2b7i   kcp-syncer-cluster1-195k2b7i-7bbc5698dc-pjm2t   1/1     Running   0          32m
kube-system                    coredns-6d4b75cb6d-6g5jz                        1/1     Running   0          4h54m
kube-system                    coredns-6d4b75cb6d-qb22j                        1/1     Running   0          4h54m
kube-system                    etcd-kind-control-plane                         1/1     Running   0          4h54m
kube-system                    kindnet-8mtcr                                   1/1     Run

kubectl --server=https://{host}/services/syncer-tunnels/clusters/<ws>/apis/workload.kcp.dev/v1alpha1/synctargets/<name>/proxy -n kube-system logs coredns-6d4b75cb6d-6g5jz

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with kcp installed or the issue environment reproducible.
